description: SnoopCatcher: a ~112-aa protein partner split from the D4 domain of S. pneumoniae RrgA that forms a spontaneous covalent isopeptide bond with SnoopTag; this instance is the G848D variant (AMK38054.1).

references.authors: Veggiani G, Nakamura T, Brenner MD, Gayet RV, Yan J, Robinson CV, Howarth M
references.title: Programmable polyproteams built using twin peptide superglues
references.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2016)

references.authors: Buldun CM, Jean JX, Bedford MR, Howarth M
references.title: SnoopLigase Catalyzes Peptide-Peptide Locking and Enables Solid-Phase Conjugate Isolation

references.authors: Keeble AH, Yadav VK, Ferla MP, Bauer CC, Chuntharpursat-Bon E, Huang J, Bon RS, Howarth M
references.title: DogCatcher allows loop-friendly protein-protein ligation

references.authors: Bruun TUJ, Andersson AC, Draper SJ, Howarth M
references.title: Engineering a Rugged Nanoscaffold To Enhance Plug-and-Display Vaccination

references.authors: Keeble AH, Wood DP, Howarth M
references.title: Design and Evolution of Enhanced Peptide-Peptide Ligation for Modular Transglutaminase Assembly

label: SnoopCatcher
note: Protein partner (~112 aa) split from the D4 (CnaA-like) domain of Streptococcus pneumoniae RrgA pilus adhesin (residues 749-860). On mixing with the peptide SnoopTag it reconstitutes the split domain and forms a spontaneous, covalent intermolecular isopeptide bond (its reactive Asn854 to the reactive Lys742 of SnoopTag), irreversibly locking the two units together. Orthogonal to the SpyTag/SpyCatcher pair. Canonical Veggiani 2016 engineered form (G842T + D848G stabilizing mutations), deposit AMD39965.1.

provenance.sequence_source: AMD39965.1 (SnoopCatcher [synthetic construct], Veggiani et al. 2016 PNAS 113:1202-1207, PMID 26787909 — the canonical engineered form carrying G842T + D848G), 112 aa; independently fetched via NCBI efetch and byte-compared (100% identity). Biological origin: S. pneumoniae RrgA D4 domain (UniProt A7KT94, residues 749-860).

functional_claims.label: Spontaneous covalent isopeptide-bond formation with SnoopTag

value.mechanism: Protein partner split from the RrgA D4 domain; on mixing with the peptide SnoopTag it reconstitutes the split domain and forms a spontaneous intermolecular isopeptide (amide) bond between the reactive Asn854 of SnoopCatcher and the reactive Lys742 of SnoopTag, covalently and irreversibly locking the two units together with release of ammonia.

source.quote: A spontaneous isopeptide bond forms in the D4 Ig-like domain of RrgA between residues Lys742 and Asn854 ... we split the D4 domain ... settled on the peptide tag we termed SnoopTag (residues 734-745) and the protein partner we named SnoopCatcher (residues 749-860)
source.section: Results (Design of an Orthogonal Covalent Peptide-Protein Interaction)

value.condition: with a twofold excess of SnoopCatcher, SnoopTag-MBP reacted quantitatively; with excess SnoopTag-MBP, SnoopCatcher was ~100% consumed

source.quote: However, with a twofold excess of SnoopCatcher, SnoopTag-MBP reacted quantitatively (Fig 1E and Fig S1B). Similarly with an excess of SnoopTag-MBP, SnoopCatcher was ~100% consumed

value.cross_reaction: none detected even after overnight incubation

source.quote: Each partner reacted efficiently with its cognate pair, but we found no trace of cross-reaction, even after overnight incubation (Fig 2C)

functional_claims.label: Reaction robust over pH 6-9 and 4-37 C, redox-independent, no specific buffer required

value.redox: insensitive to DTT (no cysteine in SnoopTag/SnoopCatcher)
value.buffer: no specific component required; works in PBS, with Triton X-100/Tween-20, or 1 M NaCl; modestly enhanced by 1.5 M TMAO

source.quote: We established that reaction proceeded efficiently from pH 6-9 (Fig 2A) and 4-37 C (Fig 2B). Cysteine is absent from SnoopTag and SnoopCatcher, so, as expected, the reaction was insensitive to DTT ... No specific buffer component was required

functional_claims.label: Engineered split-domain partner from the RrgA D4 domain of S. pneumoniae

engineered_mutations: D848G (stabilize a hairpin turn near the reaction site)
value.derivation: split-domain engineering of the RrgA isopeptide-bond-forming D4 domain into a peptide (SnoopTag) and a protein partner (SnoopCatcher)

source.quote: To optimize reaction, SnoopCatcher included the mutations G842T, designed to stabilize a beta-strand, and D848G, designed to stabilize a hairpin turn close to the reaction site (Fig S1A)

functional_claims.label: Programmable irreversible protein-protein conjugation, polyproteam assembly, and plug-and-display

applications: covalent capture of a SnoopTagged protein module
sequential / programmable polyprotein ('polyproteam') assembly when combined orthogonally with SpyTag/SpyCatcher
modular plug-and-display of antigens on multivalent nanoparticle/VLP scaffolds

source.quote: Solid-phase attachment followed by sequential SpyTag or SnoopTag reaction between building-blocks enabled iterative extension. Linear, branched, and combinatorial polyproteins were synthesized

functional_claims.label: Expressed solubly in the E. coli cytosol

value.note: SnoopCatcher was expressed efficiently as a soluble protein in the E. coli cytosol and purified by Ni-NTA affinity chromatography.

source.quote: SnoopTag fused to maltose-binding protein (MBP) and SnoopCatcher were expressed efficiently as soluble proteins in the cytosol of Escherichia coli and purified by nickel-nitrilotriacetic acid (Ni-NTA) affinity chromatography
